titleStmt.title: Letter from William Field to Augustine Birrell, 29 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William Field

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from William Field (1843–1935) an Irish politician and Irish Parliamentary Party MP for the St. Patrick’s division, to Augustine Birrell (1850-1933), the chief secretary for Ireland. The letter is written after the death of the former chief crown solicitor Sir Malachy Kelly (1850-1916). The letter concerns the application to the position of chief crown solicitor by Charles P. O' Neill (b. 1867), an Irish solicitor and member of the Irish Parliamentary Party. In the letter Field discusses O'Neill's position as as chairman of the Pembroke district of Dublin and asks that his application be given favorable consideration.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/5729-6

correspAction.placeName: Chief Secretary's Office, Dublin Castle, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
16 Booterstown Av Dublin 29 March 1916 Private My dear Mr Birrell I understand that Mr C P O Neille
is a candidate for the late Mr Malachi
O Kellys position. Mr O Neille has been
a great friend of many, having succeeded
in ousting the Tories from Pembroke
Therefore as he is fully qualified I hope
you will give his claim favourable
consideration & oblige with thanks
for past courtesy & best wishes, hoping
you are keeping well notwithstanding
the terrible weather & blizzard of criticism. Personal Wm Field
